samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Chip clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal for starting the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 16 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 16 bits. Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits. Chip output flag signal indicating the completion of the multiplication operation. Shift count register to track the number of shifts performed. <= 1'b0; Initialize the shift count register (i) to 0. <= 1'b0; <= + 1'b1; <= 1'b0; Update the shift count register (i) based on the start signal and the current value of i. Reset i to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. <= 1'b0; Initialize the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0. <= 1'b0; <= 1'b1; <= 1'b0; Update the multiplication completion flag (done_r) based on the value of i. Reset done_r to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. <= 16'b0; <= 16'b0; <= 32'b0; <= ; <= ; <= 32'b0; <= + { << ( - 1'b1), 1'b0}; Initialize the multiplicand register (areg) and multiplier register (breg) to 0 when the reset signal (rst_n) is low. Start the multiplication operation when the start signal (start) is 1. Update the multiplicand register (areg) and multiplier register (breg) based on the input signals ain and bin, respectively. Perform the shift and accumulate operation based on the value of i. If the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, accumulate the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) to the product register (yout_r). = ; Assign the product output (yout) to the product register (yout_r). <= 32'b0; <= + { << ( - 1'b1), 1'b0}; Update the product register (yout_r) based on the value of i. If the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, accumulate the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) to the product register (yout_r). <= 1'b0; Initialize the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0. <= 1'b0; <= 1'b1; <= 1'b0; Update the multiplication completion flag (done_r) based on the value of i.
